etymology.description: 先(先) (xiān, "first; previous") refers to the earlier or preceding ones in time or order. 輩(輩) (bèi, "generation; class") indicates a group of people from a particular time. Together, 先輩 (xiānbèi) refers to those who came before in a generational line, such as ancestors or forebears.
etymology.interpretation: 先(先) (xiān, "first") relates to the idea of being earlier in time or sequence. 輩(輩) (bèi, "generation") suggests a group or class of people in the context of time. Combining these gives us 先輩 (xiānbèi), referring to those who precede us in a generational sequence. As such, it naturally extends to mean 'forebears' or ancestors, emphasizing their role in preceding generations.

examples.chinese: 我们的先辈曾经生活在这里(我們的先輩曾經生活在這裡)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen de xiānbèi céngjīng shēnghuó zài zhèlǐ.

examples.chinese: 我们应该铭记先辈的贡献(我們應該銘記先輩的貢獻)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yīnggāi míngjì xiānbèi de gòngxiàn.
examples.english: We should remember the contributions of our forebears.

examples.chinese: 传统节日让我们想起先辈的智慧(傳統節日讓我們想起先輩的智慧)。
examples.pinyin: Chuántǒng jiérì ràng wǒmen xiǎngqǐ xiānbèi de zhìhuì.
examples.english: Traditional festivals remind us of our forebears' wisdom.
